hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will reduce off-target interactions with SSTR3/5 by avoiding unfavorable electrostatic contacts. These changes are expected to increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating strong binders but insufficiently differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets. Targeted mutation of non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved-pocket contacts in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions and improve SSTR2 selectivity.
Proposing mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 and improve selectivity.
